message.content:
We are building a paywalled video page called /videos using Stripe, ThirdWeb, and Mux. For our Stripe payment, let’s have it be a pop-up with Apple/Google Pay and all our branding (Helvetica titles + Arial body). Ideally, the pop-up should be like the paywalls you see out of the New York Times, Wall Street Journal, etc. We’re going to use ThirdWeb for its social sign in embedded wallet, as well as minting a simple smart contract on the backend for our membership. Finally, we’ll be using Mux to upload our videos to /videos.

Let’s create a demo page at /videos with placeholder text. It should have ‘locked’ videos for paywall that when a user clicks, it opens our Stripe pop-up. There should be a sign option via ThirdWeb in the top right corner or before the user pays via Stripe.

Let’s also create a back-end page called /uploads that enables us to upload our videos for the /videos page, including labeling title, description, and date. We’re going to enter our Env Vars last, so let’s have a working demo to ensure everything works properly. Finally, please also draft a smart contract for review that uses OpenZeppelin in case we want to edit later.

input.content:
# Paywalled Video Platform — Build Plan

## Context
Greenfield Next.js app with three integrations: **Stripe** (subscription paywall in a NYT-style overlay), **ThirdWeb** (social-login embedded wallet + on-chain membership NFT), and **Mux** (video upload/playback). Must run as a fully clickable demo **before** any env vars are set, so every integration needs a graceful mock fallback.

---

## Stack
- **Next.js 15 App Router** + TypeScript + Tailwind
- `@stripe/stripe-js` + `@stripe/react-stripe-js` (Payment Element in custom modal — *not* hosted Checkout, so we keep Helvetica/Arial branding)
- `thirdweb` v5 SDK (`ConnectButton` + `inAppWallet` for Google/Apple/Email login)
- `@mux/mux-uploader-react` + `@mux/mux-player-react` + `@mux/mux-node`
- `@openzeppelin/contracts` (Solidity source only — no Hardhat scaffolding, just the `.sol` for review)

---

## File Tree

```
package.json, tsconfig.json, next.config.mjs, tailwind.config.ts, postcss.config.mjs
.env.local.example                  ← every var documented, all optional for demo

app/
  layout.tsx                        ← <Providers> wrapper, header w/ ConnectWallet top-right
  globals.css                       ← Helvetica title / Arial body font stack
  page.tsx                          ← redirect → /videos
  videos/page.tsx                   ← grid of <VideoCard>; click locked → open paywall
  uploads/page.tsx                  ← Mux uploader + title/desc/date form
  api/
    stripe/create-subscription/route.ts   ← creates Stripe Customer + Subscription, returns clientSecret
    stripe/webhook/route.ts               ← invoice.paid → mint NFT via ThirdWeb (stubbed in demo)
    mux/upload-url/route.ts               ← creates Mux direct-upload URL
    videos/route.ts                       ← GET/POST video metadata (JSON file backend)

components/
  Providers.tsx                     ← ThirdwebProvider client wrapper
  ConnectWallet.tsx                 ← ThirdWeb ConnectButton, demo fallback = localStorage button
  VideoCard.tsx                     ← thumbnail + lock overlay; unlocked → MuxPlayer
  PaywallModal.tsx                  ← ★ the NYT-style overlay (see below)

lib/
  config.ts                         ← reads env, exports isDemoMode flags per integration
  stripe.ts                         ← server Stripe client (lazy, null if no key)
  mux.ts                            ← server Mux client (lazy, null if no key)
  thirdweb.ts                       ← createThirdwebClient + chain + contract config
  videoStore.ts                     ← read/write data/videos.json

contracts/
  MembershipPass.sol                ← ERC-721 + Ownable, mint/revoke, soulbound toggle commented

data/
  videos.json                       ← seeded with 3 demo videos (Mux public test playback IDs)
```

---

## Key Component: `PaywallModal.tsx` (NYT/WSJ style)

**Visual**
- `fixed inset-0 bg-black/70 backdrop-blur-sm z-50` backdrop
- White card, `max-w-md`, slides up on mount (`translate-y` transition)
- **Helvetica Bold** headline: *"Unlock unlimited video access"*
- **Arial** body: price + interval ("$9.99/month · cancel anytime")
- Stripe `<PaymentElement>` with `paymentMethodOrder: ['apple_pay', 'google_pay', 'card']` — Apple/Google Pay render automatically when available
- Footer: *"Already a member?"* → triggers ThirdWeb connect

**Flow gate**
1. User clicks locked video
2. If no wallet connected → show "Sign in to continue" panel first (ThirdWeb embedded wallet modal)
3. Once connected → check membership (NFT balance via `balanceOf` / demo: localStorage)
4. If not member → fetch `clientSecret` from `/api/stripe/create-subscription` → mount Payment Element
5. On `stripe.confirmPayment` success → set local membership flag, close modal, unlock videos
6. (Real env: webhook receives `invoice.paid` → mints NFT to `walletAddress` passed in subscription metadata)

**Demo mode (no `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`)**
- Modal renders identically but Payment Element is replaced by a styled fake card form
- "Subscribe" button waits 800ms → sets `localStorage.demoMembership = true` → unlocks

---

## `/uploads` Page

- `<MuxUploader endpoint={fetchUploadUrl}>` — calls `/api/mux/upload-url`
- Form: `title` (text), `description` (textarea), `date` (date input)
- On Mux `success` event → POST `{ title, description, date, uploadId }` to `/api/videos`
- API route polls Mux for asset → extracts `playback_id` → appends to `data/videos.json`

**Demo mode (no Mux keys)**
- Uploader replaced with file input that does nothing
- Form still works → saves entry with a public Mux test playback ID so it renders on `/videos`

---

## `MembershipPass.sol`

```solidity
// SPDX + pragma ^0.8.20
import ERC721, Ownable from @openzeppelin/contracts

contract MembershipPass is ERC721, Ownable {
  uint256 private _nextTokenId;
  mapping(address => uint256) public memberToken;   // 1 token per address

  constructor() ERC721("Membership Pass", "PASS") Ownable(msg.sender) {}

  function mint(address to) external onlyOwner returns (uint256) {
    require(memberToken[to] == 0, "Already member");
    uint256 tokenId = ++_nextTokenId;
    memberToken[to] = tokenId;
    _safeMint(to, tokenId);
    return tokenId;
  }

  function revoke(address member) external onlyOwner {
    // called by webhook on subscription canceled
    uint256 tokenId = memberToken[member];
    require(tokenId != 0, "Not member");
    delete memberToken[member];
    _burn(tokenId);
  }

  // Uncomment for soulbound (non-transferable):
  // function _update(...) override { require(from == 0 || to == 0, "Soulbound"); ... }
}
```

Deployed via ThirdWeb dashboard later. Backend mints with `THIRDWEB_SECRET_KEY` + contract address.

---

## Demo-Mode Detection (`lib/config.ts`)

```ts
export const demo = {
  stripe:   !process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY,
  mux:      !process.env.MUX_TOKEN_ID,
  thirdweb: !process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_THIRDWEB_CLIENT_ID,
};
```

Each integration falls back independently — you can add Stripe keys first and test real payments while Mux/ThirdWeb stay mocked.

---

## Env Vars (`.env.local.example`)

```bash
# Stripe (subscription)
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=
NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=
STRIPE_PRICE_ID=                    # recurring price, create in Stripe dashboard
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=

# ThirdWeb
NEXT_PUBLIC_THIRDWEB_CLIENT_ID=
THIRDWEB_SECRET_KEY=                # backend minting
NEXT_PUBLIC_MEMBERSHIP_CONTRACT_ADDRESS=
NEXT_PUBLIC_CHAIN_ID=84532          # Base Sepolia default

# Mux
MUX_TOKEN_ID=
MUX_TOKEN_SECRET=
```

---

## Verification

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

1. Visit `http://localhost:3000/videos` → see 3 locked demo videos
2. Click "Sign In" top-right → demo wallet connects (localStorage)
3. Click any video → NYT paywall overlay slides up with Helvetica headline
4. Click "Subscribe" → 800ms delay → modal closes → videos unlock, MuxPlayer renders
5. Visit `/uploads` → fill form → submit → new entry appears on `/videos`
6. Open `contracts/MembershipPass.sol` → review Solidity

All five steps work with **zero env vars**. Adding any one key swaps that integration from mock to real without touching code.

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# All variables are OPTIONAL. The app runs in demo mode without them.
# Add keys one integration at a time — each falls back independently.
# Copy this file to .env.local and fill in real values.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# ── Stripe (recurring subscription) ──────────────────────────────────────────
# Create a recurring Price in your Stripe dashboard, then paste the IDs here.
# Apple Pay / Google Pay are enabled automatically by Payment Element when the
# domain is registered in your Stripe dashboard (Settings → Payment Methods).
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=
NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=
STRIPE_PRICE_ID=
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=

# ── ThirdWeb (social-login embedded wallet + on-chain membership) ────────────
# Get your client ID from https://thirdweb.com/dashboard
# Deploy contracts/MembershipPass.sol via `npx thirdweb deploy`, then paste
# the contract address. The secret key lets the backend mint NFTs after payment.
NEXT_PUBLIC_THIRDWEB_CLIENT_ID=
THIRDWEB_SECRET_KEY=
NEXT_PUBLIC_MEMBERSHIP_CONTRACT_ADDRESS=
NEXT_PUBLIC_CHAIN_ID=84532   # 84532 = Base Sepolia (testnet); 8453 = Base mainnet

# ── Mux (video upload + playback) ────────────────────────────────────────────
# Get token ID/secret from https://dashboard.mux.com/settings/access-tokens
MUX_TOKEN_ID=
MUX_TOKEN_SECRET=

content.text:
---

## ✓ Running at http://localhost:3001

Typecheck, dev server, and production build all pass with zero env vars.

**Demo flow to test:**
1. **`/videos`** — 3 locked videos with blurred Mux thumbnails. Amber badges show which integrations are in demo mode.
2. **Sign In** (top right) — creates a localStorage demo wallet. Real ThirdWeb social login (Google/Apple/Email) activates when you set `NEXT_PUBLIC_THIRDWEB_CLIENT_ID`.
3. **Click any locked video** — NYT-style overlay slides up: Helvetica headline, Arial body, fake Apple/Google Pay buttons + card form.
4. **Subscribe** — 800ms processing → modal closes → all videos unlock → real Mux player renders.
5. **`/uploads`** — title/description/date form. Saves to `data/videos.json`, appears immediately on `/videos`.

**To swap demo → real**, copy `.env.local.example` → `.env.local` and fill in keys. Each integration activates independently.

**Files of note:**
- `components/PaywallModal.tsx:1` — the NYT overlay (real Stripe Payment Element + demo fallback in one file)
- `contracts/MembershipPass.sol:1` — ERC-721 with `mint`/`revoke`/`isMember`, soulbound option commented at line 67
- `app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts:1` — `invoice.paid` → mint NFT, `subscription.deleted` → revoke (stubbed, ready for ThirdWeb backend wallet)

content.text:
Code's ready. To see the real embedded wallet:

**1. Get a free client ID** (~30 sec)
→ https://thirdweb.com/dashboard → "Create Project" → copy the Client ID

**2. Create `.env.local`** with just this one line:
```
NEXT_PUBLIC_THIRDWEB_CLIENT_ID=your_client_id_here
```

**3. Restart the dev server** (env vars only load at boot)

Once that's set, both the top-right **Sign In** button and the paywall's **Sign in to subscribe** button open the real ThirdWeb modal — Google / Apple / Email / Passkey login → embedded wallet created on-the-fly → paywall advances to the payment step automatically.

---

Do you have a client ID to paste in, or should I walk you through the dashboard?

content.text:
[contracts/MembershipPass.sol](contracts/MembershipPass.sol) — OpenZeppelin v5 ERC-721 + Ownable.

**Design at a glance**

| Piece | Why it's there |
|---|---|
| `memberToken` mapping (L28) | One pass per address, enforced at L42. Doubles as O(1) lookup for `revoke` — no token-ID bookkeeping needed in your DB. |
| `mint(address)` `onlyOwner` (L41) | Stripe webhook calls this on `invoice.paid`. Owner = your backend signer wallet, so users can't self-mint. |
| `revoke(address)` (L54) | Webhook calls on `subscription.deleted`. Burns the token → `balanceOf` drops to 0 → frontend locks. |
| `isMember(address)` view (L63) | Sugar for `balanceOf > 0`. Either works from the frontend. |
| Soulbound override (L74-86, commented) | Uncomment and members can't sell/transfer their pass to a non-paying wallet. **Recommend enabling this** since access is subscription-tied. |
| `_baseURI` (L93, commented) | Optional — point at a JSON endpoint if you want a card image to show in wallets. |

**One bug to flag for resubscribe:** if a user cancels then resubscribes, `mint` will succeed (their `memberToken` was deleted on revoke), but if they're *still* subscribed and the webhook fires twice (Stripe retry), `mint` reverts — which is actually the correct idempotent behavior. ✓

**Deploy:** `npx thirdweb deploy` from the repo root → opens browser → pick chain → done. Then transfer ownership to whatever wallet your webhook signs with.

Want me to enable soulbound, or any other edits before you deploy?

content.text:
## End-to-end flow

### 1. User lands on `/videos`
[`app/videos/page.tsx:23`](app/videos/page.tsx#L23) fetches `/api/videos`
→ [`app/api/videos/route.ts:11`](app/api/videos/route.ts#L11) reads `data/videos.json` + reports which integrations are in demo mode
→ Grid renders. `isMember` is false → every `<VideoCard>` shows blurred Mux thumbnail + lock icon ([`components/VideoCard.tsx:22`](components/VideoCard.tsx#L22))

### 2. User clicks a locked video
`onLockedClick` → `setPaywallOpen(true)` ([`app/videos/page.tsx:69`](app/videos/page.tsx#L69))
→ `<PaywallModal>` mounts: dark backdrop + blur, white card slides up, Helvetica headline, $9.99/mo

### 3. Sign-in gate (ThirdWeb)
[`components/PaywallModal.tsx:107`](components/PaywallModal.tsx#L107) checks `useActiveAccount()` — none yet, so it renders the gate.

User clicks **Sign in to subscribe** → `handleSignIn` ([`PaywallModal.tsx:117`](components/PaywallModal.tsx#L117)):
- **Real mode** → `useConnectModal().connect()` opens ThirdWeb modal: Google / Apple / Email / Passkey → embedded wallet created, `useActiveAccount()` now returns an address
- **Demo mode** → `signInDemo()` writes a fake address to localStorage

Either way the component re-renders past the gate.

### 4. Payment (Stripe)
[`PaywallModal.tsx:159`](components/PaywallModal.tsx#L159) — `RealCheckout` mounts:

- `useEffect` POSTs `{ walletAddress }` to `/api/stripe/create-subscription`
- [`app/api/stripe/create-subscription/route.ts:24`](app/api/stripe/create-subscription/route.ts#L24): create Customer → create Subscription with `payment_behavior: default_incomplete` → wallet address stashed in `subscription.metadata` → return PaymentIntent `clientSecret`
- `<Elements>` mounts with brand appearance (Arial font, black primary)
- `<PaymentElement paymentMethodOrder={['apple_pay','google_pay','card']}>` → Apple/Google Pay sheets render natively
- User pays → `stripe.confirmPayment({ redirect: 'if_required' })` → on success: `grantMembership()` → modal closes

**Demo mode:** `DemoCheckout` shows fake Pay buttons + card form, 800ms wait, same outcome.

### 5. Videos unlock
`isMember` flips → `<VideoCard locked={false}>` → blurred thumbnail swapped for `<MuxPlayer playbackId={...}>` ([`VideoCard.tsx:50`](components/VideoCard.tsx#L50)). Streams immediately.

### 6. Webhook → on-chain mint *(server-side, async)*
Stripe fires `invoice.paid` → [`app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts:38`](app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts#L38):
- Verify signature with `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`
- Pull `walletAddress` from subscription metadata
- Call `MembershipPass.mint(walletAddress)` via ThirdWeb backend wallet *(stubbed — wire when contract deployed)*

On `customer.subscription.deleted` → `revoke(walletAddress)` → burns the NFT → next page load, `balanceOf` is 0 → re-locked.

---

### Upload flow (`/uploads`)
File picked → `<MuxUploader>` calls [`/api/mux/upload-url`](app/api/mux/upload-url/route.ts#L17) for a direct-upload URL → browser uploads straight to Mux → `onSuccess` enables submit → POST `{title, description, date, uploadId}` to [`/api/videos`](app/api/videos/route.ts#L23) → resolves Mux `playbackId` → appends to `videos.json` → appears on `/videos` next refresh.

---

**Demo independence** — each integration mocks itself when its env vars are missing. You can test real Stripe with mock ThirdWeb, real ThirdWeb with mock Stripe, etc.

content.text:
**Stripe** — [`app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts`](app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts)

| Event | Trigger | Handler does |
|---|---|---|
| `invoice.paid` | First (and every renewal) invoice settles | Read `walletAddress` from subscription metadata → call `MembershipPass.mint(wallet)` |
| `customer.subscription.deleted` | User cancels or Stripe ends sub after failed renewals | Call `MembershipPass.revoke(wallet)` → NFT burned |

Signature verified with `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` ([`route.ts:31`](app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts#L31)). Endpoint: `POST /api/stripe/webhook`.

**Mux** — none. Using direct upload polling instead: when `/uploads` submits, [`/api/videos`](app/api/videos/route.ts#L37) fetches `mux.video.uploads.retrieve(uploadId)` → `assets.retrieve(asset_id)` → playback ID. Could swap to a `video.asset.ready` webhook later if you'd rather not block on save.

**ThirdWeb** — none. NFT state is read live via `balanceOf` / `isMember`.

---

Worth noting: `invoice.paid` fires on **every** renewal, not just the first. The contract handles this safely — `mint()` reverts if `memberToken[wallet] != 0`, so renewal calls are idempotent no-ops. If you want quieter logs, gate the mint call on `invoice.billing_reason === "subscription_create"` ([`route.ts:42`](app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts#L42)).

content.text:
Clean. Here's the updated state machine:

| Event | Status flow | Action | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| `invoice.paid` | any → paid | **MINT** | First sub, *or* card fixed after decline. Idempotent — already-member is a no-op. |
| `invoice.payment_failed` | — | log only | Stripe runs ~3 retries over ~2 weeks. Don't double-act; the status flip below handles it. |
| `customer.subscription.updated` | active → `past_due` / `unpaid` / `canceled` / `incomplete_expired` | **REVOKE** | Card declined → access pulled immediately. They get it back the moment a retry succeeds. |
| `customer.subscription.updated` | past_due → `active` | **MINT** | Card updated mid-retry. Re-grant. |
| `customer.subscription.deleted` | terminal | **REVOKE** | Cancel-now, or retries exhausted, or period ended after `cancel_at_period_end`. |

**Key behavior:** `cancel_at_period_end=true` keeps `status=active` until the billing period ends — so a user who cancels mid-month keeps watching until what they paid for runs out. Then `.deleted` fires and we revoke. No code needed; the status check at [`route.ts:108`](app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts#L108) handles it for free.

**Stripe dashboard:** add all four events to your webhook endpoint config.

content.text:
Four things will bite you in production, roughly in order:

---

**1. The NFT is never actually checked** ← biggest gap
[`MembershipContext.tsx`](components/MembershipContext.tsx) only reads `localStorage`. When ThirdWeb is real, a returning user who paid yesterday shows as non-member. The contract exists but nothing calls `isMember(address)`.

Fix: in the context, when `useActiveAccount()` returns an address, call the contract's `isMember` view via thirdweb's `readContract`. Keep the localStorage flag as an *optimistic override* for the ~30s window between payment success and the mint tx confirming.

---

**2. `/uploads` has zero auth**
Anyone can `POST /api/videos`. For a "back-end page" you need one of: connected wallet must equal contract owner, a hardcoded admin allowlist, or a simple `ADMIN_PASSWORD` header check.

---

**3. Mux playback is public — the paywall is bypassable**
[`upload-url/route.ts:19`](app/api/mux/upload-url/route.ts#L19) uses `playback_policy: ["public"]`. Anyone who opens DevTools sees the playback ID and can watch via `stream.mux.com/{id}.m3u8` without paying.

Fix: switch to `["signed"]` policy. Add an API route that verifies membership (on-chain or via Stripe), then mints a short-lived Mux JWT with `MUX_SIGNING_KEY`. Pass that token to `<MuxPlayer playbackToken={...}>`.

---

**4. `data/videos.json` evaporates on Vercel**
Serverless filesystems are ephemeral — every write disappears on the next cold start. Fine locally, dead on deploy. Swap [`videoStore.ts`](lib/videoStore.ts) for Vercel Postgres / Supabase / even Vercel KV before going live.

---

**Smaller**
- Webhook should dedupe by `event.id` — Stripe retries, and a pending mint tx + retry = wasted gas on a second revert.
- Opening the paywall creates a Stripe Customer + incomplete Subscription even if the user closes it. Defer creation until they actually submit, or accept the 23h auto-expiry.
- ThirdWeb's `inAppWallet` exposes the user's email — pass it to `create-subscription` so Stripe receipts go somewhere.
- `PRICE_DISPLAY = "$9.99"` is hardcoded in the modal; pull it from the Stripe Price object.

Want me to fix any of these now? #1 and #3 are the ones I'd prioritize.

lines: - * Tracks membership + demo-wallet state in localStorage so the demo
- * survives page reloads. When real ThirdWeb/Stripe keys are set,
- * `isMember` should instead be derived from on-chain `balanceOf`.
+ * Membership state with three sources, in priority order:
+ *   1. Optimistic flag (localStorage) — set immediately on payment success.
+ *      Covers the ~30s gap between confirmPayment and the mint tx landing.
+ *      Polled on connect and after payment. If this says true, the
+ *      optimistic flag is cleared (no longer needed).
+ *   3. Demo wallet (localStorage) — fallback when no ThirdWeb client ID.
+  grantMembership: () => void; // optimistic — call right after payment
export function MembershipProvider({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {
+      // No contract deployed yet — chain check is a no-op, optimistic flag
+      // is the only signal. This keeps the demo working.
+      // Chain confirmed → optimistic flag served its purpose, clear it.
+    // Optimistic: unlock immediately. Chain will catch up via webhook mint.
+    // Kick off a poll so we flip to chain-confirmed as soon as the tx lands.
+      // Poll a few times with backoff — mint tx typically lands in 5-30s.
-  if (!ctx) throw new Error("useMembership must be used within MembershipProvider");
+    throw new Error("useMembership must be used within MembershipProvider");

input.new_string:
# ── Mux (video upload + playback) ────────────────────────────────────────────
# Get token ID/secret from https://dashboard.mux.com/settings/access-tokens
MUX_TOKEN_ID=
MUX_TOKEN_SECRET=

# Signing key for SIGNED playback (paywall enforcement). Without these,
# uploads use public playback IDs and anyone with the ID can watch.
# Create at https://dashboard.mux.com/settings/signing-keys
# MUX_PRIVATE_KEY is the base64-encoded private key Mux gives you.
MUX_SIGNING_KEY=
MUX_PRIVATE_KEY=

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# All variables are OPTIONAL. The app runs in demo mode without them.
# Add keys one integration at a time — each falls back independently.
# Copy this file to .env.local and fill in real values.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# ── Stripe (recurring subscription) ──────────────────────────────────────────
# Create a recurring Price in your Stripe dashboard, then paste the IDs here.
# Apple Pay / Google Pay are enabled automatically by Payment Element when the
# domain is registered in your Stripe dashboard (Settings → Payment Methods).
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=
NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=
STRIPE_PRICE_ID=
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=

# ── ThirdWeb (social-login embedded wallet + on-chain membership) ────────────
# Get your client ID from https://thirdweb.com/dashboard
# Deploy contracts/MembershipPass.sol via `npx thirdweb deploy`, then paste
# the contract address. The secret key lets the backend mint NFTs after payment.
NEXT_PUBLIC_THIRDWEB_CLIENT_ID=
THIRDWEB_SECRET_KEY=
NEXT_PUBLIC_MEMBERSHIP_CONTRACT_ADDRESS=
NEXT_PUBLIC_CHAIN_ID=84532   # 84532 = Base Sepolia (testnet); 8453 = Base mainnet

# ── Mux (video upload + playback) ────────────────────────────────────────────
# Get token ID/secret from https://dashboard.mux.com/settings/access-tokens
MUX_TOKEN_ID=
MUX_TOKEN_SECRET=
